source: DFM-REFERENCE wish list + dream allocation; HITL gates and agent flow per tool

utilization_summary.benefit_beyond_sheet: Agentic AI delivers the dream allocation by automating coordination and manual checks while keeping humans in the loop. HITL gates ensure quality and control at each tool.
role_highlights: Producers: Coordination 74.5% → 41%. Freed time → Client Strategy 10%, Creative QC 12%, Innovation Lab 10%.
Production Coordinators: Coordination 70% → 40%. Highest automation ROI. Freed time → Proactive Asset Mgmt 17%, QC 5%.
Creative Directors: Preproduction 29% → 50%. V1 Review, ClickUp, Post-pub → 0%. More ideation, scripting, packaging.
Post-Production Managers: Reactive 1:1s → 30% intentional editor management. Video Review 28% → 35%. Hiring+Onboarding 15%.

utilization_table.benefit: Freed time → Client Strategy 10%, Creative QC 12%, Innovation Lab 10%.
Highest automation ROI. Freed time → Proactive Asset Mgmt 17%, QC 5%.
V1 Review, ClickUp, Post-pub → 0%. More ideation, scripting, packaging.

hitl_gates.description: Human confirms task title, list, due date, assignee, tags (e.g. Mango, Lemon), and dependencies before ClickUp create_task is called.
Producer reviews created task in ClickUp.

PR-4.reasoning: Agent parses natural language from LLM text input (replaces Slack): extract task name, list/space, due date, assignee, tags (e.g. Mango, Lemon), dependencies. Resolves list name to list_id via workspace hierarchy.
PR-4.planning: 1) Parse user text (LLM or fallback). 2) get_workspace_hierarchy or get_list to resolve list_id. 3) Optionally resolve_assignees. 4) HITL: human confirms. 5) create_task with name, description, tags, due_date.
PR-4.executing: Calls ClickUp create_task with name, list_id, description, tags, due_date. Agent logs task to ClickUp after HITL confirm. Input is LLM text box (replaces Slack).

tool_calls.purpose: User types task in plain language; agent parses to extract name, due date, tags (Mango, Lemon), dependencies.
Resolve list/space from user intent (e.g. 'Mango Pod' → list_id).
Create task with title, list_id, description, tags, due_date, dependencies in description.

outputs_by_stage.output: Parsed: task_name='Script review by Fri', list=Batch 14 — Longform, assignee=editor.d, due=Friday.
Resolved list_id=li_100101 (Batch 14 — Longform).
Human approved. Proceeding to create_task.
Task created. task_id=…, url=https://app.clickup.com/t/…

utilization_estimate.benefit_beyond_sheet: Agentic AI reduces manual task creation and batch setup; HITL keeps control. Freed time → Client Strategy (+14%), Creative QC (+9%), Innovation Lab (+10%).

hitl_gates.description: Human confirms suggested assignee(s) before ClickUp update_task (assign) is executed.
PC/PPM verifies assignment in ClickUp; editor notified via Slack.

ED-3.reasoning: Agent evaluates incoming task: content type, complexity, estimated hours, pod. Queries capacity store and ClickUp list_tasks to get workload per editor. Ranks by skill match and available capacity.
ED-3.planning: 1) get open tasks from ClickUp list. 2) Query capacity/skill store (or internal DB). 3) Rank editors by fit. 4) Present top N to human. 5) On confirm, update_task assignees.
ED-3.executing: Calls ClickUp MCP get tasks for list; merges with capacity data; suggests assignees. HITL gate: human picks/confirms. Then update_task to set assignees.

tool_calls.purpose: Fetch open tasks and current assignees to compute workload.
Hours available, shortform vs longform skill per editor.
Set assignees on task after HITL confirm.

outputs_by_stage.output: Open tasks in list: 8. Editor A 12h, Editor B 5h, Editor C 22h, Editor D 40h available.
Best match: Editor D (40h, longform 4.0). Alternative: Editor C (22h, longform 3.5).
Human confirmed Editor D. Proceeding to assign.
Task LF-1403 assigned to editor.d. Slack notified.

utilization_estimate.hours_saved_per_week: PC: ~64h (40%→20% ClickUp); Editors: ~97h idle reduction (6.5%→2% of 2160h)
utilization_estimate.benefit_beyond_sheet: Agentic assignment distributes workload; HITL prevents wrong assign. Editors spend less time waiting; PCs free time → Proactive Asset Mgmt (+17%), QC (+5%).

hitl_gates.description: CD reviews predicted retention curve and drop-off flags before sharing with writer/team.
CD may override or accept suggested structural edits.

CD-1.reasoning: Agent ingests script + channel context; infers segment boundaries and viewer behavior model. Identifies likely drop-off points and low-engagement sections.
CD-1.planning: 1) Load script (Notion/chat). 2) Call retention/analytics model (or API). 3) Generate curve + flags. 4) Suggest edits with rationale. 5) Present to CD.
CD-1.executing: Tool: retention model or ViewStats/YouTube API. Output: curve data, timestamps, suggestions. HITL: CD approves before briefing creator.

tool_calls.purpose: Fetch script text and channel context.
Predict retention curve and drop-off flags.

outputs_by_stage.output: Script loaded; 4 segments, ~3 min total.
Curve generated. Drop-off flags at 0:45, 2:10. Suggestions: shorten intro, add hook at 1:30.

utilization_estimate.benefit_beyond_sheet: Agentic retention analysis shifts CD time from manual analysis into ideation and scripting; supports 30% faster time-to-first-draft KPI.

hitl_gates.description: CD may review digest summary before it is sent to Slack or team.
CD can filter or re-categorize outlier themes.

CD-2.reasoning: Agent monitors ~20 competitor channels; detects outlier performance (theme, format, timing). Triangulates and categorizes for weekly digest.
CD-2.planning: 1) Fetch competitor data (YouTube/ViewStats API). 2) Compute outliers. 3) Categorize by theme, format, timing. 4) Format digest. 5) Slack MCP or webhook.
CD-2.executing: Tools: YouTube Data API or ViewStats; Slack MCP. Output: digest text + optional link. HITL: CD can approve before broadcast.

tool_calls.purpose: Fetch performance data for competitor channels.
Send weekly digest to CD or channel.

outputs_by_stage.output: 20 channels fetched; 5 outlier videos identified.
Digest: 5 outliers. Top theme: how-to; format: 8–12 min; timing: Tue–Thu.

utilization_estimate.hours_saved_per_week: Part of CD preproduction 29%→50%; research automated
utilization_estimate.benefit_beyond_sheet: Outlier ideation automated; more time for creative decisions and packaging. Agentic research replaces manual competitor checks.

hitl_gates.description: CD reviews score and thumbnail text candidates before sending to designer.
CD may adjust curiosity-gap phrases for brand.

CD-3.reasoning: Agent analyzes thumbnail + script; scores on curiosity gap, clarity, click-worthiness. Extracts phrases for thumbnail text; cross-references search demand.
CD-3.planning: 1) Vision model for thumbnail. 2) Script → phrase extraction. 3) Google Trends/SEO if available. 4) Score + candidates. 5) Present to CD.
CD-3.executing: Tools: vision API, script parser, optional Trends API. Output: score, candidates. HITL: CD approves brief.

outputs_by_stage.output: Thumbnail score: 7.2/10. Strong hook; text legibility OK.
Candidates: 'The Truth About X', 'Why X Matters'. Trends: 'X' rising.

utilization_estimate.hours_saved_per_week: Part of CD preproduction packaging shift
utilization_estimate.benefit_beyond_sheet: Faster thumbnail strategy; clearer briefs for designers. Agentic packaging supports 50% preproduction target.

hitl_gates.description: CD may configure which competitor changes trigger Slack alert (threshold, channels).
CD reviews before/after and delta; decides next action.

CD-4.reasoning: Agent detects title/thumbnail/description changes on competitor channels; correlates with performance delta. Surfaces only meaningful changes.
CD-4.planning: 1) Poll competitor channels (YouTube/ViewStats). 2) Diff titles/thumbnails/descriptions. 3) Get performance before/after. 4) If delta significant, Slack alert.
CD-4.executing: Tools: YouTube Data API, ViewStats; Slack MCP. Output: before/after, delta. HITL: CD acts on alert.

tool_calls.purpose: DM or channel alert with before/after.

outputs_by_stage.output: Channel Y: 2 title changes, 1 thumbnail. Delta: +12% CTR.
Slack alert sent to CD with links and delta.

utilization_estimate.benefit_beyond_sheet: Competitor A/B monitoring automated; CD time reallocated to preproduction. Agentic surveillance replaces manual channel checks.

hitl_gates.description: When sentiment drops below threshold, human reviews video-level and client-level summary before escalating.
PPM may adjust threshold or exclude certain clients from alerts.

ED-1.reasoning: Agent ingests Frame.io comments; runs sentiment model. Aggregates per video and per client. Flags when below threshold.
ED-1.planning: 1) Frame.io API for comments. 2) Sentiment model. 3) Aggregate video + client. 4) Compare to threshold. 5) If low, Slack alert.
ED-1.executing: Tools: Frame.io API, sentiment model; Slack MCP. Output: summary, trend, alert. HITL: PPM reviews before action.

outputs_by_stage.output: Video X: 12 comments. Client Y: 3 videos this week.
Video-level: 2 neutral, 1 positive. Client trend: stable. No alert.

utilization_estimate.benefit_beyond_sheet: Sentiment visibility reduces reactive 1:1s; PPMs spend more on intentional editor management and hiring. Agentic monitoring surfaces risk early.

hitl_gates.description: PPM or QC lead reviews timestamped report before sending to editor; may waive minor items.
Severe violations may require human decision to block publish or request re-export.

ED-2.reasoning: Agent runs video through checklist (intro/outro, lower-third, audio, color, b-roll, captions, export). Flags violations with timestamp and severity.
ED-2.planning: 1) Load video + checklist (ClickUp/Notion). 2) QC service (internal or 3rd party). 3) Timestamped report. 4) Present to PPM.
ED-2.executing: Tools: QC service API; ClickUp/Notion for checklist. Output: report with timestamps. HITL: PPM approves before sending to editor.

tool_calls.purpose: Run checks and return timestamped violations.

outputs_by_stage.output: Checklist: 5 categories, 12 items.
3/5 passed. Violations: 0:00–0:30 intro; 2:00 b-roll gap.

utilization_estimate.hours_saved_per_week: PPM Video Review 28%→35% (quality up); Editors Revisions 13%→8%
utilization_estimate.benefit_beyond_sheet: Automated QC reduces manual review burden; fewer revision cycles. Agentic checklist execution catches issues before CD review.

hitl_gates.description: CD may review score and roast before sending to Slack (if not instant command).

CD-5.reasoning: Agent scores title + thumbnail on curiosity gap, clarity, click-worthiness; generates roast-style feedback.
CD-5.planning: 1) Vision model for thumbnail. 2) Score + roast. 3) Slack response.
CD-5.executing: Tools: vision API; Slack MCP. Output: score (1–10), roast text. HITL optional for batch use.

tool_calls.purpose: Send score and roast to channel.

outputs_by_stage.output: Score: 7/10. Roast: 'Hook is strong; text legibility could improve.'

hitl_gates.description: CD may approve or refine query before agent queries knowledge base.

CD-6.reasoning: Agent uses client-specific Claude Project or Notion KB; returns on-brand suggestions and references.
CD-6.planning: 1) Query + context. 2) Claude Project or Notion MCP. 3) Format response.
CD-6.executing: Tools: Claude Project or Notion MCP. Output: on-brand suggestions. HITL: CD uses output in ideation.

tool_calls.purpose: Query client KB for brand voice and past decisions.

outputs_by_stage.output: On-brand suggestions; 3 past decisions referenced.

hitl_gates.description: CD or writer reviews AI-rewritten script before handoff.

CD-7.reasoning: Agent matches draft script to client voice using previous scripts and cadence examples.
CD-7.planning: 1) Draft script + voice examples. 2) LLM rewrite. 3) Output script.
CD-7.executing: Tools: LLM; Notion for script storage. Output: rewritten script. HITL: human approves final.

tool_calls.purpose: Rewrite script to match client voice.

outputs_by_stage.output: Script rewritten; cadence and vocabulary aligned to client.

hitl_gates.description: CD posts title/hook in Slack; agent responds with score (instant). Optional: CD may filter which channels get bot.

CD-8.reasoning: Agent scores title/hook using channel-specific historical data; returns 1–10 and optional rationale.
CD-8.planning: 1) Slack message triggers. 2) Predictive model. 3) Reply in thread.
CD-8.executing: Tools: Slack MCP (receive); predictive API; Slack (reply). Output: score. HITL: CD acts on score.

outputs_by_stage.output: Score: 8/10. Posted in Slack thread.

hitl_gates.description: CD may filter or prioritize outlier dashboard before sharing with team.

CD-9.reasoning: Agent aggregates outlier content per client niche; maps to content calendar.
CD-9.planning: 1) Client/niche config. 2) YouTube/ViewStats. 3) Dashboard or Notion.
CD-9.executing: Tools: YouTube/ViewStats API; Notion or dashboard. Output: client-contextualized outlier list. HITL: CD reviews.

outputs_by_stage.output: 5 outliers this week; mapped to content calendar.

hitl_gates.description: Producer confirms timeline and milestone dates before writing to ClickUp.

PR-1.reasoning: Agent computes schedule from content type, deliverables, complexity, review turnaround, capacity.
PR-1.planning: 1) Inputs (type, count, complexity). 2) Calculator logic. 3) Output schedule. 4) Optional: ClickUp create.
PR-1.executing: Tools: internal calculator or Make/n8n; ClickUp MCP. Output: proposed schedule. HITL: Producer approves then apply.

outputs_by_stage.output: Proposed schedule: 4 milestones, buffer 2 days.

hitl_gates.description: Producer reviews sentiment shift and decides whether to escalate or reach out.

PR-2.reasoning: Agent aggregates Slack, email, Frame.io per client; builds sentiment profile; flags shifts.
PR-2.planning: 1) Ingest comms. 2) Sentiment model. 3) Profile + alerts.
PR-2.executing: Tools: Slack MCP, Gmail API, Frame.io API; sentiment model. Output: profile, trend, alert. HITL: Producer acts on alert.

outputs_by_stage.output: Client Y: 12 messages; sentiment stable. Client Z: trend down; alert.

hitl_gates.description: PC or Producer confirms templated message and variables before Slack send.

PR-3.reasoning: Agent fills template with client/project variables from ClickUp or Notion; sends via Slack.
PR-3.planning: 1) Template + trigger. 2) Resolve variables (ClickUp/Notion MCP). 3) Send Slack.
PR-3.executing: Tools: Slack MCP; ClickUp or Notion MCP for variables. Output: message sent. HITL: Human confirms template and vars.

outputs_by_stage.output: Variables: client=Mango, project=Batch 14. Message ready.
